sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses multiple questions regarding disclosure and disqualification requirements for a California Horse Racing Board member regarding: (1) a quarter horse partnership investment and stud fee income; (2) potential conflicts from a home-building business whose clients might own racehorses; and (3) late filing of a Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that the quarter horse partnership does not require disclosure under Category 2 (racehorse breeding), but income over $250 annually triggers disqualification duties; the home-building business requires disclosure only if clients are known sources of $10,000+ income and are covered by Categories 1 or 2; and a $700 late-filing fine was imposed for the 5-day delay in filing the Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, numbered; Skipped (too short): question; QUESTION has only 0 words; LLM: Document has significant OCR degradation/formatting issues with numerous incomplete words and missing text. The question section exists but is fragmented. The conclusion/advice is dispersed throughout the response sections rather than consolidated. The document addresses Questions 1 and 2 with detailed factual scenarios and responses. Late-filing fine section is clearly identifiable. Confidence reduced due to text quality issues, though core advice is discernible.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The document addresses multiple questions regarding disclosure and disqualification requirements for a California Horse Racing Board member regarding: (1) a quarter horse partnership investment and stud fee income; (2) potential conflicts from a home-building business whose clients might own racehorses; and (3) late filing of a Statement of Economic Interests.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that the quarter horse partnership does not require disclosure under Category 2 (racehorse breeding), but income over $250 annually triggers disqualification duties; the home-building business requires disclosure only if clients are known sources of $10,000+ income and are covered by Categories 1 or 2; and a $700 late-filing fine was imposed for the 5-day delay in filing the Statement of Economic Interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-82-071 addresses disclosure and disqualification obligations for Douglas R. McAvoy, a California Horse Racing Board member, regarding investments in a quarter horse stud partnership and a home-building business, while also imposing a $700 late-filing penalty for his delayed Statement of Economic Interests.
